page: Microsoft Azure E16-4ads_v5 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the EADSv5 server family. It is powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 virtual CPUs, derived from 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. It features 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32 GB per vCPU. Local storage is bundled, providing 614 GB of local disk capacity, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. It runs under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark results reveal a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% in stress-ng single-core tests with a score of 2702.68. However, due to the low core count, multi-core performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core tests. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for large-block read operations but weak performance for small-block reads and large-block writes. Database and web serving benchmarks, such as Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput, also rank in the bottom 25%.

The E16-4ads_v5 offers a highly specialized resource density profile. By pairing 128 GB of RAM with only 4 vCPUs, it provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require high compute parallelism. This design avoids the cost overhead of licensing or provisioning excess vCPUs simply to acquire substantial memory. The primary tradeoff is the limited multi-threaded processing power and lower memory write speeds. This server is suited for memory-intensive applications with low parallel processing demands, such as small-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-heavy single-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E16-4ads_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the EADSv5 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 614 GB of local storage. While single-core CPU performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, the low core count results in weak multi-core and database performance, as evidenced by low Geekbench multi-core and Redis SET benchmark scores. This server offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads that do not require extensive parallel processing, allowing users to access large memory capacities without paying for unneeded vCPUs. It is best suited for memory-intensive applications with low compute parallelism, such as caching layers, small in-memory databases, and single-threaded data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16-4ads_v5 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 4 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core performance but limited multi-core capacity.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16-4ads_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A highly memory-dense virtual machine pairing substantial RAM with low vCPU overhead for memory-intensive, low-parallelism workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 4 vCPUs and 2 physical cores
High memory density of 128.0 GB RAM providing 32 GB per core
Bundled 614 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core CPU performance but weak multi-core scaling
Suited for caching layers and small-scale in-memory databases
